title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today's signal centers on how AI safety work is becoming more operational: new system documentation, targeted red-teaming incentives, and research on failure modes that slip past current safeguards. The strongest updates focus on multi-turn jailbreak risk, privacy tooling, and evaluation methods that better expose memorization, grounding, and security blind spots.

items.title: Transient Turn Injection exposes stateless multi-turn jailbreak vulnerabilities
items.why_it_matters: Shows how turn-by-turn moderation can miss distributed harmful intent across conversations.

items.title: OpenAI publishes GPT-5.5 system card
items.why_it_matters: System cards are primary evidence for how frontier models were evaluated, mitigated, and deployment-scoped.

items.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty
items.why_it_matters: Expands external red teaming for high-consequence bio misuse and jailbreak discovery.

items.title: OpenAI releases Privacy Filter for PII detection and redaction
items.why_it_matters: Operational privacy filters can reduce accidental exposure of sensitive personal data in AI pipelines.

items.title: CrossCommitVuln-Bench targets vulnerabilities invisible to per-commit analysis
items.why_it_matters: Highlights evaluation gaps in code-security systems that miss risks emerging across multiple changes.

items.title: Revisiting non-verbatim memorization in LLMs through entity surface forms
items.why_it_matters: Improves how researchers measure memorization, with implications for privacy, leakage, and reliability claims.

items.title: Grounding Video Reasoning in Physical Signals introduces a benchmark for localized physical understanding
items.why_it_matters: Tests whether models truly ground answers in evidence rather than exploiting dataset shortcuts.

items.title: MathDuels proposes self-play evaluation for models as both problem posers and solvers
items.why_it_matters: Offers a new way to differentiate advanced model capabilities beyond saturated static benchmarks.

items.title: FAccT-Checked reviews how AI is reconfiguring authority in journalism
items.why_it_matters: Frames accountability shifts when AI mediates editorial decisions, responsibility, and epistemic trust.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
